parameters.name: selection

parameters.name: emplacement

parameters.name: action_target

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Minimum software version required for the feature

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: Feature capability that must be allowed by server

parameters.name: user_type
parameters.description: Type of user account

parameters.name: action_type
parameters.description: Method to activate the feature
parameters.example: activate interphony from Settings / Telephony / Auto-answer

parameters.name: device_state
parameters.description: Required state of the SIP phone
parameters.example: in a call with another phone

parameters.name: call_scenario
parameters.description: Call scenario to be executed
parameters.example: other phone makes a second call to a different phone

representative_sentence: From version {version}, if {capability} is allowed by OXE server for {user_type}, {action_type} or ensure {device_state} and {call_scenario}.
notes: Examples describe two different scenarios: 1) Activating interphony feature via settings/progkey, 2) Setting up a specific call scenario. No single clear action matches both examples from the allowed list.

parameters.name: loop_count

parameters.name: start_step
parameters.description: Step number where the loop begins

parameters.name: end_step
parameters.description: Step number where the loop ends

parameters.name: action_during_loop
parameters.description: Action to perform during each loop iteration

notes: This cluster describes a test loop structure with embedded actions rather than a single VoIP action. The example contains both loop control ('Loop 10 times from step 3 to step 7') and an action ('change the volume level'), making it a compound instruction that doesn't map cleanly to a single standard action.
